diagrams.description: Art History studies visual and material culture across time and place. This flowchart outlines its disciplinary logic: the ultimate goal is to interpret the meanings, functions, and contexts of art, and to understand its role in human societies. It integrates core components—periods and styles (from Prehistoric to Contemporary), core concepts (iconography, style, authorship, patronage), and contextual domains (religious, political, social, economic). Analysis draws on diverse sources and methods (visual analysis, archival research, technical examination, provenance studies) and is framed by major theoretical lenses—formalism, iconology, social history of art, feminist and gender studies, postcolonial and global art histories. These guide the interpretive process (description → analysis → interpretation → evaluation) and yield outcomes such as cultural heritage preservation, critical visual literacy, and deeper understanding of human creativity and identity.
